data.title: Almonty Industries Closes Oversubscribed US$800 Million Convertible Notes to Fund Tungsten Expansion
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Almonty Industries ( (TSE:AII) ) just unveiled an update.

On June 9, 2026, Almonty Industries Inc. closed a significantly oversubscribed US$800 million offering of 2.25% convertible senior notes due 2031, including the full US$100 million over-allotment exercised by initial purchasers. The private Rule 144A placement generated net proceeds of approximately US$772.7 million, with about US$83 million allocated to capped call transactions designed to limit dilution from potential note conversions.

The remaining funds are earmarked for working capital and general corporate purposes, including potential acquisitions, giving Almonty a substantially stronger balance sheet. Management said the financing underpins development of the Sangdong Mine and supports the company’s push to emerge as a leading non-China supplier of strategic tungsten to Western defense and advanced technology customers, a positioning that could be increasingly important amid heightened geopolitical and supply-chain tensions.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:AII) stock is a Buy
with a C$25.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Almonty Industries stock,
see the TSE:AII Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on AII Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AII is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by very weak profitability and ongoing cash burn despite improved revenue growth and gross margin. Technicals are mixed (near-term weakness but longer-term trend support), while valuation is constrained by loss-making earnings and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on AII stock,
click here.

More about Almonty Industries

Almonty Industries Inc. is a multinational tungsten producer focused on supplying conflict-free material to defense and advanced technology sectors in Western markets. Its flagship Sangdong Mine in South Korea, historically one of the world’s largest and highest-grade tungsten deposits, anchors a portfolio that also includes operations in Portugal and projects in the U.S. and Spain.

Positioned as a key non-China source of this strategic metal amid tightening export controls and defense-related procurement bans, Almonty aims to reinforce supply-chain security for the United States and its allies. The company targets growing demand for tungsten used in armor, munitions, and electronics, leveraging its assets to address critical vulnerabilities in Western industrial and military supply lines.

Average Trading Volume: 887,051

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: C$6.75B

Corvus Pharmaceuticals ( (CRVS) ) has shared an update.

On June 11, 2026, Corvus Pharmaceuticals held its 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, with 73,021,854 shares represented out of 84,090,424 outstanding as of the April 16, 2026 record date. Stockholders elected Richard A. Miller, M.D., and Linda S. Grais, M.D., J.D., as Class I directors to serve until the 2029 Annual Meeting or until their successors are in place.

Investors also ratified the selection of PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as Corvus’s independent registered public accounting firm for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2026. In addition, stockholders approved, on a non-binding advisory basis, the compensation of the company’s named executive officers, signaling broad support for Corvus’s current leadership and governance practices.

Angi ( (ANGI) ) just unveiled an announcement.

At its June 10, 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders, Angi shareholders elected three Class II directors—Sandra Buchanan, Thomas C. Pickett Jr., and Glenn H. Schiffman—to serve until the 2029 annual meeting or until their successors are chosen, reinforcing continuity in the company’s board leadership. Shareholders also approved the 2017 Stock Plan and ratified Ernst & Young LLP as Angi’s independent registered public accounting firm for the 2026 fiscal year, signaling investor support for the company’s compensation framework and financial oversight structures.

The director elections drew varying levels of support, with Pickett receiving the highest number of votes and Schiffman the lowest, while all three secured sufficient backing to remain on the board. Approval of the stock plan and the auditor ratification, both supported by strong majorities, underscore stockholder confidence in Angi’s governance practices and its approach to aligning executive incentives with long-term shareholder interests.

data.title: La Rosa Faces Nasdaq Compliance and Equity Deficiency Challenges
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

La Rosa Holdings Corp. ( (LRHC) ) has provided an announcement.

La Rosa Holdings Corp. reported that on May 21, 2026 it was notified by Nasdaq that it was out of compliance with listing rules due to delinquent filings of its Form 10-K for 2025 and Form 10-Q for the quarter ended March 31, 2026. After the company filed its Form 10-K on June 4, 2026, Nasdaq informed La Rosa on June 10, 2026 that it had regained compliance regarding the annual report, but it remains noncompliant because the Form 10-Q is still outstanding.

On June 11, 2026, La Rosa submitted a compliance plan to Nasdaq, which may grant up to October 12, 2026 for the company to regain full filing compliance, and the company said it intends to file the Form 10-Q as soon as practicable. Also on June 10, 2026, Nasdaq notified La Rosa that its negative stockholders’ equity of $(1,848,252) as of December 31, 2025 put it below the $2.5 million minimum required, triggering a separate deficiency notice; the company has until July 27, 2026 to submit a remediation plan and potentially until December 7, 2026 to restore equity levels, while evaluating options to address the shortfall and avoid a possible delisting.

Spark’s Take on LRHC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, LRHC is a Neutral.

LRHC scores low primarily due to weak financial performance (widening losses, persistent negative cash flow, and negative equity) and bearish technicals (price below key moving averages with negative MACD). Corporate events add uncertainty from Nasdaq filing deficiencies and going-concern/internal-control issues, while valuation metrics provided are not informative (P/E 0.00; no dividend yield).

To see Spark’s full report on LRHC stock,
click here.

More about La Rosa Holdings Corp.

La Rosa Holdings Corp. is a Nevada-based company whose common stock trades on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol “LRHC.” The company is subject to Nasdaq’s continued listing requirements, including timely filing of periodic reports with the SEC and maintaining minimum stockholders’ equity thresholds for its listing status.

data.title: Athena Technology Extends Business Combination Deadline into 2027
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

The latest update is out from Athena Technology Acquisition Corp II Class A ( (ATEK) ).

On June 11, 2026, Athena Technology Acquisition Corp. II stockholders approved an amendment to extend the deadline to complete a business combination from June 14, 2026 to as late as March 14, 2027, via up to nine one‑month extensions funded by deposits from its sponsor into the trust account. The same day, 99.75% of eligible Class A shares were represented at the virtual meeting, the extension proposal passed unanimously, redemptions totaled 11,313 shares for about $138,565, and on June 12, 2026 the company deposited $271.48 to trigger the first one‑month extension to July 14, 2026, providing more time to secure a suitable deal while modestly reducing trust funds.

MainPoint1: Athena Technology Acquisition Corp. II obtained stockholder approval on June 11, 2026 to extend its business combination deadline from June 14, 2026 to as late as March 14, 2027 through up to nine sponsor-funded monthly extensions.

MainPoint2: At the virtual meeting, the extension passed unanimously with high turnout, a small number of investors redeemed shares for about $138,565, and the company funded the first extension to July 14, 2026, slightly trimming its trust account while gaining more deal time.

More about Athena Technology Acquisition Corp II Class A

Athena Technology Acquisition Corp. II is a special purpose acquisition company that raised capital through an initial public offering of Class A common stock and warrants. The company’s primary objective is to identify and consummate a business combination, using a trust account structure that allows public stockholders to redeem shares for cash if they choose not to participate in a proposed deal.

data.title: Avista Pauses Data Center Talks Amid Grid Concerns
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

An update from Avista ( (AVA) ) is now available.

On June 12, 2026, Avista announced it has paused processing energy service negotiations for a proposed 500 MW data center, citing the need for a broader, coordinated planning process with government agencies and stakeholders amid heightened community concern. The move underscores the growing strain that large-scale data center projects place on regional grids and signals Avista’s intention to balance economic development with customer protection, system reliability and regulatory oversight, as it works with local leaders to shape how future large-load requests are evaluated.

Avista emphasized that existing customers will not bear the costs of serving any new large customer, that reliability-driven engineering studies and upgrades are mandatory before service, and that any eventual agreement would require regulatory approval. By framing the pause around transparency, community engagement and responsible growth, the company is positioning itself to manage escalating data center demand while maintaining public trust and aligning with broader regional priorities.

The most recent analyst rating on (AVA) stock is a Hold
with a $42.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Avista stock,
see the AVA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on AVA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AVA is a Neutral.

AVA scores in the mid-range driven primarily by stable financial performance typical of a regulated utility, but constrained by leverage and historically volatile free cash flow alongside softened revenue trends. Valuation is a support (moderate P/E and strong dividend yield), while technical signals are neutral-to-soft and the earnings outlook is balanced by ERM, regulatory, and financing/capex execution risks.

To see Spark’s full report on AVA stock,
click here.

More about Avista

Avista Corp. is a regulated energy company engaged in the production, transmission and distribution of electricity and natural gas, along with other energy-related businesses. Through Avista Utilities, it serves 429,000 electric and 386,000 natural gas customers across eastern Washington, northern Idaho and parts of southern and eastern Oregon, and also provides electric service in Juneau, Alaska, via a subsidiary.

data.title: Genasys Secures High-Interest Term Loan for Liquidity
data.description:
Introducing TipRanks MCP for Agents

  • Deliver institutional-grade market data directly into Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, and other MCP-compatible AI tools.
  • Designed for personal research, portfolio monitoring, and AI-assisted investment workflows.

Genasys ( (GNSS) ) has provided an update.

On June 9, 2026, Genasys Inc. entered into and closed an unsecured term loan agreement with Maran Partners Fund, LP, securing $4.3 million in financing to be used solely for working capital and general corporate purposes. The loan carries an 18% annual interest rate, matures on September 14, 2026, and requires monthly interest payments, with an origination fee of $301,000 deducted at closing and an additional exit fee ranging from $64,500 to $150,500 depending on the repayment date.

The agreement includes customary representations, warranties, and restrictive covenants that limit Genasys from certain distributions, new indebtedness, asset sales, redemptions, and fundamental changes, and it mandates prepayment upon events such as a change of control, significant non-ordinary-course asset sales, or certain equity issuances. While Genasys may prepay the loan without penalty in $250,000 increments with 30 days’ notice, the high fixed interest rate and tight covenants underscore both the company’s near-term liquidity needs and the lender’s emphasis on control and downside protection, factors that may influence Genasys’s financial flexibility and strategic options through the September 2026 maturity.

The most recent analyst rating on (GNSS) stock is a Buy
with a $6.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Genasys stock,
see the GNSS Stock Forecast page.

Avalo Therapeutics ( (AVTX) ) has provided an update.

On June 11, 2026, Avalo Therapeutics entered into an exchange agreement with an accredited investor to swap 4,294.675 shares of its Series C non‑voting convertible preferred stock for an equal number of newly created Series C‑1 non‑voting convertible preferred shares, designed to mirror the prior terms but allow the investor to raise its beneficial ownership cap from 4.99% to 9.99% of common stock, leaving 4,085.379 Series C shares outstanding and relying on private offering exemptions. On the same day, the company filed a certificate of designation in Delaware to establish the Series C‑1 Preferred Stock, which is convertible into common shares subject to a 9.99% ownership limit, ranks on parity with existing preferred and common stock, and includes broad-based anti‑dilution protections, while on June 12, 2026 Avalo amended employment agreements for its top executives to enhance severance, change-of-control protections, equity vesting terms, and tax-efficient treatment of potential golden parachute payments.

data.description:

OptimizeRx ( (OPRX) ) has shared an announcement.

At its June 9, 2026 annual meeting, OptimizeRx shareholders approved an amendment to the company’s 2021 Equity Incentive Plan, raising the pool of common shares available for equity awards by 1,000,000 to a total of 5,450,000, while rejecting a proposed evergreen feature that would have allowed automatic annual share increases. Shareholders also re-elected seven directors, gave advisory approval to executive compensation, and ratified Grant Thornton LLP as the independent auditor for fiscal 2026, signaling broad but not unconditional support for management’s governance and compensation framework.

The meeting’s voting outcomes underscore investor willingness to expand equity-based incentives, which may aid talent retention and alignment with shareholders, while the rejection of the evergreen provision reflects caution about potential future dilution. The ratification of the auditor and re-election of the board provide continuity in oversight, suggesting that investors favor stability but intend to keep tighter control over how additional equity capacity is deployed over time.

The most recent analyst rating on (OPRX) stock is a Buy
with a $11.00 price target.
